title: Encoded Digits
story: [Meridian-7 Log #008] rEx uses a numeric encoding system prefixed with "0x" — strikingly similar to human hexadecimal format. Perhaps mathematics truly is the universal language. Extract all valid rEx numeric values from the data stream.
promptChallenge: Write a prompt to have AI generate a regex that matches hexadecimal numbers (e.g., 0xFF, 0x1A2B)
hints: [Hint] Valid hexadecimal characters are 0-9 and A-F (including lowercase)
[Hint] At least one hexadecimal digit is required
promptHints: Describe the prefix and allowed characters of hexadecimal numbers
Tell the AI the valid range is [0-9a-fA-F]
Clarify that the 0x prefix is mandatory

feedback.onPass: [Pattern Accepted] Encoded values decrypted. The numeric rules are becoming clear.
feedback.onFail: [Parse Failed] Encoding recognition failed. Hexadecimal rules are stricter than you think.
feedback.onPerfect: [Decryption Success] Precise extraction. rEx's numeric encoding has nowhere to hide.
feedback.onDirectWrite: [Manual Override] Manual decoding. You read hexadecimal directly.
